title: Koanophyllon triradiatum
taxonomy: Koanophyllon triradiatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Koanophyllon. The plant is native to Mexico, and its classification is within the tribe Eupatorieae. The classification of Koanophyllon triradiatum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae. The plant's classification is also recognized in the order Asterales.

intro: Koanophyllon triradiatum, also known as "three-rayed koanophyllon," is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Koanophyllon triradiatum is used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon triradiatum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including pain management and fever reduction.
